reviews.review_text:
Easily the best survival game I've played since Valheim. I regret sleeping on this title for so long even though the premise interested me when it first launched. A part of me is glad I waited because now I get to enjoy it as a complete package rather than waiting for content to release over time. I haven't been this excited to play a game in a while and I really can't wait to play Grounded 2 once I'm finished with this game completely. 

I bought this game hoping for an open world survival game with a fairly linear story so I could choose whether or not I wanted to advance the plot and have some level of guidance or spend time exploring the world. This game nailed that balance perfectly. There's always some objective to do if that's what you want but otherwise the game often encourages you to sprawl out across the lawn and discover things organically. The exploration harkens back to the Obsidian days of old. If you are curious and care to explore the world you will be rewarded. Every. Damn. Time. The game has so much stuff to see and do. I could rave about all the various features and mechanics that make me surprised newer survival titles haven't borrowed from Grounded but I will say that the game is very thoughtful in making the boring elements of base building and resource management as painless as possible so you can spend more time in the world doing what you enjoy. 

I will also say this is a game that does difficulty right which seems very rare nowadays. Our friend group started on Whoa (Hard) difficulty and it genuinely makes you fearful of some of the larger foes in the game. You learn to become avoidant and plan out your trek away from your base. You also learn when you're starting out as a new player where the safer spots are and where to avoid. It gives you a sense that you're actually surviving in the world and eventually you learn to adapt and conquer these challenges. That being said, the game also does a great job of never making you too comfortable. Just when you feel confident in your ability to fight something like Bombadier Beetles, Stinkbugs, and Orb Weaver Spiders, the story progression introduces new threats and challenges to humble you.  Another nice touch is the game tends to scale enemy damage and health with the amount of players actively on the server so it's not just a complete cakewalk when you get your friends involved. If you are unprepared for a fight you walk into and you choose to aggro a large insect anyways there's a strong chance you will die even with a group. You still have to play smart and help each other out in fights and the emphasis on teamwork and planning makes the co-op that much more fun to me. 

I bought this on sale but having played it I would say it's very easily worth the $40 asking price. Over 70+ hours into the game and there's still tons of content, places to explore, and things to do. It's a great game I would recommend to anyone.
its getting more and more obvious, i can deny it no longer, i am small

Being a lifelong gamer, this game very quickly became one of my top favourite games of all time. The nostalgia, the "Honey, I Shrunk the Kids" vibes, the unique crafting and combat, what's not to love? 

In survival crafting games I love exploring and finding the perfect place to build my base, then decorating and perfecting it while venturing out to find new places to see and fun things to do. I feel this game is perfect for that, as well as appealing to my friends that play with me who prefer to perfect their combat skills. The enemies scale very well to the player's power and equipment level and provide challenging but satisfying encounters all the way to the end. As someone who considers myself bad at combat in games, I didn't find the enemies too difficult even in singleplayer either. 

The multiplayer has worked flawlessly for me, and I love that I have the ability to get onto a world and upgrade our base even when my friend isn't online. 

I adore the different landscapes throughout the yard, the unique crafting materials and new things to see in each area, how they made each section of the yard feel like an entirely new and alien world despite the entire game taking place in a suburban backyard. Truly incredible game design. The enemies are also balanced and tuned to each biome as I said before so they all feel like they fit both in the world realistically and in a game progression sense, they scale very well to the player's skill ability. Taking down a black ox feels like a superb accomplishment the same way taking down a wolf spider makes you feel on top of the world in early game.

The story is engaging and kept me invested in figuring out what the heck happened to me and the people around me, but not so overbearing that I felt like I didn't have time to explore the nooks and crannies of the yard or like I was being rushed to the next story point or milestone. 

I also really love the character design, the human designs are all unique and stylish but the bugs are also designed in a way that ranges from adorably cute to super threatening and scary looking. As an artist I can appreciate how difficult it is to make a bug design appealing but this team has blown it out of the park with each and every species. I feel the aphids are particularly charming. 

Overall this very easily became one of my most loved games of all time and I'll gladly peer pressure any one of my friends into playing it either with me or solo just to experience it. I couldn't recommend this game more!

Amazing devs added detailed key rebind options, something I really appreciate and rarely gets done well. Big win for left handed players.
Great game. Great survival craft, great ambiance. Works pretty okay with high latency connections, but not perfect.
Been playing both multiplayer and single player. Very addicting. One minute its 8pm and you're sitting down for 30 or so minutes of play and 20 minutes later its 2am. Would recommend.

I've played a bunch of different survival craft-a-thons, and I have to say this is probably one of my favorites, if not THE favorite.

Ground has something of a story explaining the circumstance for why your chosen teenagers is not 2 inches tall, and sets up a serious of goals to guide you through the game. The direction makes the experience a lot more focused, and between the missions and a tidy crafting tree, it's easy to progress without feeling too on rails. 

The yard is nicely put together, and with enough time it's easy to navigate without using the map too much. A lack of mounts or vehicles can make traveling a little tedious, but the map is small enough it rarely becomes too tedious. 

The scale is the best part, really. Blades of grass are building sized, ladybugs as large as cars. Really easy to get immersed in everything. SPIDERS ARE VERY LARGE AND VERY SCARY LOOKING, YOU HAVE BEEN WARNED XD. 

Difficulty isn't too bad to start, though as you get further along enemies do get quite a bit tougher. You won't really get to a point where you can stomp everything you encounter, but the games gives you enough resources to keep yourself competitive. And if you are looking for more challenge, you can select a harder difficulty. 

Multiplayer works just fine also. My partner is on Xbox and can just hop in my games without issues. No lag notice in over 25 hours of play; everything feels stable. 

If the wide open structure of your typically survival game doesn't pull you in much, Grounded may help you ease into the genre without being too overwhelmed.

Funny game for a group of friends or couples. Build your little houses, fight insects and craft equipment just like Minecraft and Terraria. Story wasn't so interesting and traveling around the map took too long so we got bored by the end but it was fun regardless.

8/10
one of the best games ever but grounded 2 isnt as good trust me grounded 2 is dogwater compared to this
Great game. 4/5. Plenty to do and explore. Fun take on the genre. A couple key discoveries are a little too hidden.
Very well made and put together, the combat and exploration are lovely and the multiplayer works well despite some problems in earlier versions.

Enjoyed the game so much that I clocked an ungodly amount of hours on it within the first 3 days of starting, and now finally completed for the first time with a 100% report card, 10/10 experience.

Primarily played on Deck with no issues, mostly 60fps on Medium settings, FSR Balanced.

I'm actually surprised how good this game is, lol.
Played in Early Access and it was a pretty sweet concept, the yard wasn't finished but I still had a fun time.
Now the game has been completed and I am just blown away! 
I don't leave many reviews but this game deserves praise.
Amazing game I played it when it first dropped on Xbox and then got it on steam and went through to get the 100%. 10/10 would recommend
Great game to play with family and friends id say this is like 4+ in age rating but if youre older its still fun!
A fun and rewarding game, I love the accessibility options.
Beautiful game just so frustrating sometimes. i see why only 5-ish% of the fanbase actually beat the game.
great game, very fun, at the early game its a bit scary but once you get used to the game it becomes fun

this game is so good. I can't sing it's praise enough. so I will quickly list off what you need to know.

Combat: Simple but fun to do. once you know how to perfect parry, the Backyard is your domain. not hard enough? oh their is one Boss in this game that will give you that challenge...

Base budling and Crafting: you can built almost anywhere you want except in labs. my tip, when tarting out, make your first base on the lily pads. it's safe and leave plenty of room to built  if you know what to do. crafting better gear is always a blast

Story: no spoilers but for a first time playthrough, just listen to all the tapes you find. it helps give you context to the environmental story telling and shows the slow decline of the pore scientist . the Teen's themselves are quite likable. Peter is my fav to play, I like his voice lines

I could list other things like it's music being amazing, or exploration is fun to do, but ill leave it their. please pick up this game if you can. if you have friends to play it with, then you will have a much more fun experience. well done to Obsidian for making this. I played this game in early access on Xbox, and I can say this game came a long way from it's humble beginning. please play this game.
Great game for the kids that used to do CIA torture methods to roly-polies in their backyard

After finishing this game, I could say it's the best and most enjoyable game I've ever played. If you enjoy exploring, fighting, building, and upgrading weapons and tools, then you should try it! It is a great game with a great theme and story!

I made so many memories playing this game, we built a base on top of the baseball that overlooked the backyard and had ziplines going everywhere, we also had fun launching ourselves with the firework launcher and I had so much fun playing the game.

11/10 experience
A truly anamazing game would recommend. So much to do and so much fun reminds me of old fashioned game design. Just focus on game play and a unique idea not monitization.

I was not expecting this game to be as deep as it was. I still havent 100% beat the story to this game but id say im about 80% of the way there. This game is deeeeeep. I didnt expect it to be as in depth as it was but Im happy I found this little gem of a game. Its so good. Upgrading feels like it comes at a decent pace so it never really feels like I was forced to do certain things. Combat felt good, bosses felt tedious but not in an annoying way but more like a "I gotta beat this" dark souls sort of feeling. If you like exploration, survival crafting and fighting bosses with friends, this game is a must try imo. The experience feels very flushed out. My only takeaway is traversing the world can be harsh in the early game but once you get upgraded, things become easier. Id easily give this game a solid 7.3/10. Extremely fun
Beautiful art style, fun gameplay, great both with friends and solo.
good game but the last 1/4 dragged and the balancing got really bad at the end. Seemed like you needed more people with how much damage and health late game enemies have.

I like almost every aspect of this game! Really the only downside is the Ressource Analyzer, which feels redundant.

Furthermore, i usually only play as Maxwell, since he doesn't complain when eating a granola bar, or honeydew... every other kid complains about that, and it gets annoying quick

if youve ever fully played through hollow knight youll know what im talking about
getting past the hedge lab is just like getting past forgotten crossroads, its just a little boring or difficult but after that the game really picks up and its just an absolute masterpiece
Things with more than 4 legs shouldn't exist. Making things with four legs bigger than me shouldn't exist. Maybe this game shouldn't exist. But its fun so I'll give it a pass.

One of the most fun and unique gaming experiences you can have. When I started playing video games over 20 years ago I could not imagine the world that is possible to play in Grounded.

The crafting and combat has enough complexity that all the enemies and weapons offer something different to keep gameplay fun and fresh.

The greatest strength of this game comes from the exploration and fighting bugs. The game forces the player to really overcome more and more dangerous threats to progress. The exploration, gear, and combat reinforce a fun and satisfying loop of exploring, fighting, and returning to base.

The world is like no other game I have played has offered. I highly recommend to almost anyone.

P.S. If you are afraid of the spider (which are very scary) you can turn on different levels of arachnophobia mode to remove spider-like features.

Very competent survival crafter.
Being very small bring a new dimension to how the world feels

Dang, im thinking back to my playthough in 2024 with my brothers friend, it was peak building, defending, fight and grinding, this game is the perfect mix of everything you could want in a game. If i could do it all again I would I highly recommend this game!

Highly addictive story based wilderness survival. Progression is comfortably difficult. Overall style and attention to detail is impeccable. 
Genuinely ive been so locked in on this game. The environments spark a curiosity that keeps me playing. The base building capabilities are seemingly endless and its so much fun. (Tho as an arachnaphone the high tier spiders are terrifyingly fast.) My only complaint is that the in game plushies arent as fluffy and cute as id hope they would be.

To this date, i think it is one of the best survival-crafting game out there. So much to explore, interesting story and definitely has a difficulty to it lol. I recommend it even on full price, Grounded will always have a special place in my heart.
Amazing game i have spent months on one save and i still cant get enough!!!

I had a ton of fun playing the game during early access. Sadly my entire save was deleted and I have zero aspirations to rebuild the kingdom I constructed. Outside of potential save vulnerability this game offers tens of hours of fun some instability lots of ants and

Its really fun and interesting how the devs designed the armor and weapons and buildings off of the actual resources in the game but if we are all being for real.... WE ARE NOT SURVIVING LIKE THESE KIDS ARE
Goated survival game w friends its 100% worth its money and is a super good story/survival game

This game does survival crafting right. Many games have tried to fit into this genre but none as creatively as grounded. The quirkiness of the concept hides a uniquely scary take on the genre. I will always love Pete, the voice in my head saying "Raw Science" and the feeling of getting parry master III without even knowing.
10/10

This game was great. The whole thing was really fun, it's well optimized, and the gameplay feels very good. The graphics look good without turning your graphics card into mush. The multiplayer also works quite well, and it has cross platform as well. Definitely recommend!

This Is a game that completely understands what's so good about survival craft games. Lots of charm and creativity. The gameplay is just engaging enough to keep you on your toes. The most surprising thing of all to me is that that story is pretty engaging, and perfectly complements the world of Grounded, and the genre. This is the best survival craft game I've played, and it's not even close. Also a fantastic game if you're a bug enthusiast.
A really fun and well made game. A lot of stuff to do. Playing multiplayer with it is a blast.

Easily one of the best survival games I've played. It is a perfect combination between story, building and adventure!
It does have some issues though. Apparently pressing the E-key is optional. It makes it hard for the game to be immersive because I'm cursing at a granolabar mid adventure. Whenever I play multiplayer my duo has to pick it up. Only after he drops it, can I pick it up. 
The rate at which larva spawn and attack us is crazy. What is even crazier is that they have the audacity to complain about our presence!
Oh and apparently mosquitos love usurping because my house is currently been taken over by two mosquitos who have ganged up with a wolf spider.

Want to thank the developers for making an amazing game. 

I played it with my two nephews. It was so much fun.

Exploring different labs with such a variety of scenery. From The Blueberry tree adventure to swimming under water looking for clues while finding pockets of air. Every lab was so unique and fun. 

The game is made well so adults who like parry mechanics can enjoy the game while my younger newphews could overcome that difficulty with just better armor that they really enjoyed farming for.  Such a well made and well thought out game. Thank you to the developers for this wonderful little world they made. Pun intended :)

Great game, only issue is that if I want to play multiplayer I'm not able to just open the game and play. I have to make sure both Steam and the game are closed out, run Steam as admin, and open the game from Steam, and we get about 2 hours max before we start getting booted out randomly. Game still worth the issues though.
This game is pretty fun. I like the aspect a lot, and the survival and creatures are cool as well. I would recommend buying if it is on sale.

In my top 5 greatest games of all time. Such a great premise, executed so phenomenally. If you aren't terrified of bugs (and maybe if you are lol) give this game a try. It's a game I can play over and over with friends.

Highly recommend!

Be careful with this one. I thought I'd just be playing one quick little playthrough but now it's been 100 hours and I've 100% completed the game and I somehow feel nowhere close to being done with the game. 
It's way too easy to fall in love with this game so play at your own risk.

On a real note I played this game for many hundreds of more hours on Xbox since it went public in 2020 and every time I replay it I fall in love with it all over again.
please play this game
Fun game but the missions are not super clear, fun to play with friends 8/10
9/10 Survival game, i Love everything about this game, except it's silly uncanny art style. if you can get past that however you're in for a very well put together game!
Grounded was great, story was interesting, remixed was epic, overall the game is 9.3/10. enjoyed the mutations and community aspects of this game as well. Performance is good overall.
I played in beta and they switched some things around so this is my second go around, but overall the gameplay had me hooked, I loved the mechanics, overall a stellar game, excellent for friends.

Great to play with friends, and has a very nice game environment. It runs very smooth, it looks good, the story is fun, and the 80s/DND vibes feels like an instant classic of a game. I'm a huge fan of the building, the grind, the boss fights, etc. This game can be played in many different ways. I've got several hundred hours on Xbox.

I played more on the xbox, which is how I found the game so play hours aren't true. My review however is true, and this game is a blast. I enjoyed Minecraft but haven't picked it up since my teen years and this is scratching that itch and then some. Don't come into this expecting GTA, Dark Souls, etc. It's got action, it's got quests, it's got base building and exploration. That's all I need and the best part is it's enjoyable in solo or party mode and you can have different saves/worlds for each.
Gave this game a try during the first few years of release but ended up getting bored. Restarted with my girlfriend and it is so much fun for the both of us

I love this game! I was skeptical when I saw the buzz a few years back, but I am pleased to to be surprised. I actually got hooked because some friends were starting up with Grounded 2 and I didn't want to be left out; I am so so glad that I did get pulled in because Obsidian did a fantastic job with a rich, immersive world. Do it. Do it. You'll love it.
Good game, I loved everything about it. Felt a bit text heavy in some places, but I think it's ok for an average Joe
I honestly love this game purely because of the aesthetic, the whole 'nineties teen' vibe with everything.

I love this game I am an older dude and well this just reminds me of honey I shrunk the kids sooooo much I love the fact that the game looks friendly but it actually full of dangers hahaha So far so good I love it !
It's lots of fun and exciting. An adventure that's worth it in the end.

As I'm nearing the end of the story, I can confidently give the game a thumbs up. 

It's a stunning survival/adventure game, where everything you build, research and fight has purpose in moving you forward as you explore the vastness of the backyard. 

Being a huge fan of the film this is clearly inspired by, exploring the backyard was a joy where huge set pieces dominate the landscape and that feeling of awe when a 30 seconds journey for you or I is a 20 minute walk when you're as small as an ant. 

The combat is satisfying - learning enemy movesets allows you to parry perfectly and each creature has a set of weaknesses and strengths which means you can/should tailor your loadout on the fly to even the odds. 

The story was very good for a survival game as well and all in all, it was/is a very enjoyable game. 

My main gripes would be that, playing solo, the base building is GRINDY! Same as most other survival games as a solo that don't offer resource tweaks, gathering for building and even gear can require multiple trips and with no mounts or fast travel, it got to be a bit of a slog at times with all the back and forth. 

The same goes for making sure you're equipped for literally every situation, all of the time - my inventory towards the end was a mess of gear and clutter as I never knew what weapon I'd need to use at the time, would I need torches, do I need gear for swimming, did I need a gas mask, bombs? Who knows! To save the long trip back to your base when you find a piece of loot or a new area that needs "x" item, I felt forced to carry so much on my, just to keep the game flowing at a decent pace. 

Gripes aside, I think even after all this time, this game holds up well and is very much worth your time. 

8/10!
It`s fun, nice survival game, weapons are decent. Overall I like it.  :)

Grounded is easily one of the best games for bonding with friends. From the moment you shrink down into the backyard, the game forces you to rely on each other, creating a unique connection that solo play just can't replicate. It constantly keeps you on your toes; one minute you're gathering resources, and the next you're running for your life from a spider or a wolf spider, making every session thrilling.

The gameplay loop involves a satisfying amount of grinding to upgrade your gear and survive the harsher environments, but it never feels tedious because the progression is so rewarding. What really sets it apart, though, is the story. The mystery of what happened to the kids and the world around you makes discovering the next section incredibly engaging. You'll find yourself eager to explore just to uncover the next piece of the puzzle.

I highly recommend this game, but with one major caveat get people to play with. While it's playable alone, the experience is exponentially better with a group. If you have friends ready to dive in, this is a must play
A campy understanding of the early nineties and narrative. I can only hopelessly recommend this gem. Like all great games, this also has community support!
Fun Overall, but its best to make the spiders look less like spiders before playing

Grounded:

Is one of the best survival games I have played, and I would recommend it to almost everyone.

The idea is simple but brilliant: you are shrunk down to the size of an ant and have to survive in a backyard full of giant insects. What would normally be normal grass, puddles, spiders, ants, and ladybugs suddenly becomes a huge and dangerous world. The setting is creative, fun, and easy to get lost in.

The gameplay loop is excellent. You explore, gather resources, craft gear, build bases, fight insects, unlock better equipment, and slowly push into more dangerous areas of the yard. The combat is fun, the building is satisfying, and the progression always gives you something new to work toward. It can become repetitive at times, but it is the good kind of repetitive where you still want to keep playing.

The story is also better than I expected for a survival game. It gives the world purpose without getting in the way of the exploration and crafting. The graphics, music, atmosphere, and characters all help make the game feel polished and memorable.

Going for 100% completion was fun, which is rare for survival games. There is a lot to do, but it feels rewarding instead of painful.

Overall, Grounded is fun, addictive, creative, and packed with charm. Whether you like survival games, co-op games, base building, exploration, or just want something different, this is absolutely worth playing.
Played it on Game Pass before removing Game Pass, best QoL features out of any game, 4th favorite survival game.
It's interesting. Its like honey I shrunk the kids, and now they are 100% smaller than a ant. Never look at life this way before, but i sure would hate to be tiny. A toaster may struggle to run this.

Fantastic game, super fun to play with friends and family. Crossplay can be unstable in my experience, PC and xbox, if the host crashes everyone gets kicked, it would have been nice to have dedicated server support.  That being said the game is totally worth playing.

Stunningly beautiful game, amazing job on atmosphere and details, ai and map design. Game is a bit overwhelming tho, easy to get lost, or not know where to go or what to do next. That said I am really enjoying the game, and you will too. In a sense it reminds me of when I first played minecraft. Lots to do and explore, this game just has a higher difficulty curve

THIS GAME IS AWESOME!! i cant wait to play the second game! i play this game with my partner alot and started on xbox! we almost 100% the game but then i swapped to PC and we had to start over but its worth it cause i REALLY like this game!! i hope the 2nd game has cooler furniture!! :3

(if there is ever a 3rd game i hope we get character customization!!)

Very promising story. Very unique environment and gameplay. Kinda blends Base Building and Monster Hunter in a unique and varied setting. Not beat it yet, but looking forward to play it in coop with my friends.

A great game. Those that grew up in the 90s and were born in the 80s will have a strange nostalgia from 'Honey I shrunk the kids' film. It's fun, a little cartoonish and fairly easy survival mechanics. I would certainly call his more a crafting sandbox over a survival game as the penalties of failing survival are very light. 

While I did enjoy the multiplayer, I preferred the single player experience.
This is my favorite survival game, and easily in my top 5 games of all time. To me, it's a masterpiece. Buy it on sale, not on sale... it doesn't matter, you'll love it.

One of the top survival games of all time. The yard is amazing, the atmosphere as well. I never imagined I would like a game with bugs this much. Gameplay wise - this game provides a ton of different and unique mechanics, freedom to build and explore and hours of replayability. The bosses are balanced and very fun to fight (over and over again). The story is interesting and not urgent - you can just spend all your time building, although you will have to progress at some point to unlock new stuff. The game is equally fun with friends or alone. I LOVE the funny little quips the characters say from time to time. The Playground gamemode gives you the freedom to create whatever you imagine with the game's assets. Overall the game is a 9/10 for me and i definitely recommend it! It is worth the money and even makes you appreciate the real life buggies a little more.

Terraria 3D but with a parry system, instant 9/10, specially with friends. 
One month of your life will be pretty much gone if you dig in, so massive recommend. 

That said tho, parkour sucks like wet-trash (Massive patience is required at times), some compeling weapons-armors-builds-items have missing potential due to underwhelming numbers/effects and the construction mechanics could use some massive tweaks (Like destroyed buildings leaving their blueprint for re-construction or materials not being scatered after dissasembeling a structure). All that aside, if you can get past that and corny story moments then this is a must play if you're into a challenging survival experience that comes acompanied with an enveloping atmosphere, pretty visuals and liking for min-maxxing your character's power.

8.9/10

This game is definitely in my top 10 open world survival crafting games that I love to play. The combat is fun and sometimes unforgiving. The building mechanics are perfect. The resource grind is just right. I will be getting to NG+ 10.

I didn’t expect much from Grounded going into it, but I ended up being surprised. Grounded is a fun little survival game that gives you many different cool weapons and playstyles, and on top of that there’s also a parrying mechanic that you’re almost required to learn. I ended up using a life-steal dagger for most of my playtime because I loved getting my health back from those spider attacks.

At the beginning of the game, almost anything will kill you super easily. The Wolf Spider feels like an enemy that’ll always be hard to fight, but towards the end all those enemies aren’t even a threat anymore because you become so strong during your journey. The design team also put in a lot of work because the game looks cartoonish while still being really artistically beautiful. I didn’t care much about the story, but the gameplay was amazing.

The boss fights are also really good, especially the optional ones. I don’t want to spoil anything there, but they’ll definitely give you some trouble.

One of the things I didn’t like was the base defenses, since every wall in the game gets destroyed so easily and then you have to rebuild everything, which sucks when playing solo.

The 100% didn’t overstay its welcome, but the collectibles kinda did, which is why the 100% gets a few point deductions.

Game: 8/10

Very detailed game, great story line, fun to play.  Unique progression with choices.  Some grind, but it is minimal compared to many games I have played.  Attacks on your base are interesting, but progressively more difficult, especially with flying insects.  Overall a very captivating game.

Still love the game, 
love the principal, love the story, the enemys, the weapons, the armors, 
the snarky comments form the Characters.

And even the parts that i don't like that much ain't half as bad as mothers brussels sprouts.
Thank you Obsidian Entertainment for this game. Excited to play the second part with my pals.

Recomend the game to everyone who loves the idea of living as miniature in a normal sized world (and also everyonde who hates bugs) this game was and is still pure entertainment for me and my friend.

I recommend this because i think .
A the combat is amazing.
B the bosses are very creative.
C the graphics, designs and overall feel of the game is the best ive seen.
And i'm not the first to say it. Though i do think this could be an even bigger seller if this and the sequel game combined there mechanics features and even maps.

funish to play in coop but there are much much more fun games to play in coop then this. gameplay is repetetive and becomes annoying quite fast, liked ant pets and fun arachnofobia mode. story is kinda bland in my opinion, base building is abysmal in terms of grinding, and crafting armors and stuff even more tedious.
Very fun, open world, good with friends, ton of fun, lots to do, i cant get bored!
Super epic and cool game, but the big giant bugs scare me and my boyfriend keeps carrying me only halfway through the game and then we restart months later. I'll update when we finally beat the game.

Highly addicting and the crafting and building mechanics are very well done and makes the game feel very well balanced. You can get very overpowered with the right builds and playing crossplay with friends is a very big bonus.
What is there to say? A fun survival game with a nice theme, and ability to cheese your way through fights if you're smart :)
Good game, I really liked it! Spend 75 hours in steam and more than 20+ hours on Microsoft Store.
Overall great experience and lots of fun, interesting as single player or/and Family game. 10/10 would recommend. Sometimes unclear, but plenty of resources online ;)

Really innovative take on the genre. Don't let the cute 'honey I shrunk the kids' nostalgic 90s playfulness fool you - this is still very much a survival game and it can be equal parts zany fun and brutal adrenaline-rush.
Honestly, I've never seen so many bugs in a game, maybe something for the devs to consider when updating the second game? Otherwise, runs perfectly. One of the best survival crafters out there

If you like world exploration, upgrading, base building, melee combat and (according to all the other reviewers) Subnautica, I would highly recommend trying this game. It was really fun beating it both times since you can miss a lot if you aren't checking every inch of the map (yes, that's a good thing).
Very rewarding and adjustable to many difficulties.  Building is solid exploring is fun.

Really fun and engaging game, when you think that surely you have explored everything possible and know the backyard inside out you'll stumble upon a whole new section of the backyard with new dangers and discoveries. It's so big I've been building bases in many different corners of the backyard because it's practically impossible to run back and forth from one single base to offload loot, make food and sleep etc (even with the help of ziplines -and building high enough for those is a pain but also so fun and rewarding when you get to zip across parts of the backyard). The only thing that could possibly make this game any more fun and engaging for me would be the ability to create and customize your own character. 

Also... as someone with arachnophobia (who ISN'T using the arachnophobia setting, mind you) you best believe there have been many sudden shrieks of terror ripped out of me when a wolfspider has snuck up on my happily unaware self. Screamed right in my brother's ear when we were chatting on the phone... oops. I guess this is my version of playing a horror game -knowing complete terror is potentially awaiting me around every corner yet soldiering through with my heart in my throat and tears in my eyes. I am such a brave little potato lol. 10/10 game so far, definitely recommend it for anyone as long as you like cozy base building, adventuring and survival mixed in with bits of horror.

Simple, straightforward fun with friends. If that's what you're after this is it.

Don't be fooled into thinking this is a short game even in single player it's long specially if you want to explore all that it's got. 

Recommended.

My favorite game of all time. The progression, gear, building, exploration, and boss fights are all fantastic, and everything you would want in a game even for someone who doesn't exactly love the generic survival gameplay loop. Although you're still cutting "trees" and collecting food, the way it is done is so refreshing and clever even now with over 500 hours in the game.

 I'm so glad I got this game for the first time right before the final updates because I know that early access was.. underwhelming. Sad to see what that has done to completely wreck the opinion of this game by people who got burnt out by the early lackluster content. I also got a few hours into Grounded 2 and have now dropped it because it already seemed to have that effect on me. 

In its current state Grounded is a true gem, and people who played it in early access should definitely give it another go as the polished version is truly a masterpiece.

Grounded turns a simple backyard into a massive adventure packed with charm, creepy bugs, and addictive co-op survival gameplay. The crafting and exploration are top-notch, though the grind and occasional jank can wear thin. Still, if you love survival games with friends or solo, this one’s an easy recommendation. 10/10!
played and streamed this game for a long time during early access and had a blast doing it
I couldnt even get the game to run I had a pretty good computer. Actually unplayable. I wish I could say something bad about the actual game but I couldnt even play it actual unplayable sorry.

I want to like this game, and I really like the concept of it quite a bit.  Unfortunately this game is riddled with probably one of the worst combat systems that I have ever experienced.  

Bugs glitch frequently, team up on you and are just very unfriendly?  Like I don't mind a difficult game per se, but I also don't want to pour all of my waking hours trying to learn how to fight a mob.  There is a very steep learning curve to the game as the combat system is pretty centered on perfect parrys to avoid taking damage, but one mis step causes a quarter to a third of your hp to be lost, on top of being poisoned, while I feel like I have to slap enemies for a nauseatingly long time to kill them.  
 
I wouldn't necessarily mind that if I could just whittle them down but they heal up to full once they exit combat meanwhile your weapons and armour break.  It's rather frustrating as I lucked out into a tier two weapon and found after killing a dozen or so mobs, it would just break and I would need to hunt a tier two enemy to find the mats to repair them, causing the weapon to be unusable.  

I want to like the game and I'm probably just bad, but I really think there's just so many systems that punish you negatively to really enjoy the game without diving deep into it.  If you like that type of stuff, then you might enjoy it but it's such an unfriendly game that I just bounce off of it everytime. The concept of a tiny kid roaming around in a backyard is super fun, but the gameplay demands way more than I'm willing to put in.

I played this a bunch in early access, recently tried to play again and ran into several repeatable unplayable bugs:

Loaded a new game, changed settings, closed the settings window: Black screen, had to quit and start a new game
Loaded a new game, instant crash to desktop
Loaded a new game and yay, didn't crash this time. Needed to change settings again, black screen again. Tried to save so I could at least skip over the new game setup, got the error 'Can't save, no player character loaded' so I quit and restarted
Loaded a new game, instant crash to desktop again

I give up, I can't imagine actual game play is more stable and I don't have any interest in trying further, seriously annoying
